ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON JUN-04-2015 IN DEPT. 503, PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS MODIFIED: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR PREFERENCE IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 36(A) AND (D). THE TRIAL DATE IS SEPTEMBER 28, 2015 AT 11:15 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 503. THE 120TH DAY IS OCTOBER 2, 2015. THE COURT WILL ALLOW A MAXIMUM OF FOUR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTIONS PER DAY TO BE CALENDARED IN THIS ACTION. SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IN ROOM 603 AT 9:00 A.M. ON SEPTEMBER 24, 2015. FURTHER CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE ON JULY 16, 2015 AT 1:30 P.M. THERE WILL BE A MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE WITH THE COURT PURSUANT TO SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULES OF COURT RULE 5 ON SEPTEMBER 28, 2015, AND/OR ON A DATE BEFORE JURY HARDSHIPS, AT 1:30 P.M. IN DEPARTMENT 503. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, COURT REPORTER: SANDY LEE OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR 	971

Plaintiffs' Motion For Preference (Add To Calendar For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only).
Off Calendar Jun-08-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: (Ruling: continued from previous page)

Further case management conference on July 16, 2015 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on September 28, 2015, and/or on a date before jury hardships, at 1:30 p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by September 24, 2015 at 2:00 p.m. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Court Reporter: Sandy Lee of AIKEN WELCH, CSR 971 (Tel: 510-451-1580). (503/TLJ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Jun-04-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Gilbert Purcell of BRAYTON PURCELL LLP 415.898.1555, appearing for plaintiffs /moving parties Vester Fortner and Lois Fortner.
James Sinunu of SINUNU BRUNI LLP 415.362.9700, appearing for defendant / opposing party Western Plumbing & Heating Company, Inc.
Andrea J. Caslett of MCKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE LLP 415.267.4094, appearing for defendant / opposing party Certainteed Corporation.
Evanthia M. Spanos of BERRY & BERRY 510.250.0200, Designated Defense Counsel.
(Refer to the sign in sheet for additional appearances.)

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF.

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(a) and (d). The trial date is September 28, 2015 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. The 120th day is October 2, 2015. The Trial Case Management Order dated May 7, 2014 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is September 17, 2015. Plaintiffs stipulate that summary judgment/adjudication motions can be brought on 28-day notice with opposition due 14 days, and reply 7 days, before the hearing date. The last day for filing summary motions is August 20, 2015. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Before the Court will hear any discovery motion the parties involved in the discovery dispute must schedule and attend an informal discovery conference with the Court. The deposition of plaintiff is to be completed by July 8, 2015. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than July 10, 2015. Responses to the demand are due no later than July 17, 2015. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by September 21, 2015 at 3:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:00 a.m. on September 24, 2015. Fact discovery cut-off date is September 11, 2015. Expert discovery cut-off date is September 25, 2015. No later than June 19, 2015 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than June 19, 2015 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. (continued)
